rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Resolve 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors by correcting root reference issues or removing invalid guarantee clauses.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the same annex or component scope.
Validate that AGREE scalar type names match AADL expectations (int, real, bool) to prevent resolution errors.
Align helper declaration types with the types of their assigned expressions to avoid assumed vs actual type mismatches.
Remove invalid AGREE clauses instead of adding architecture when references cannot be resolved.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL component scopes: type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
AGREE property associations and references must not introduce identifiers not declared in the AADL model or its configured dependencies.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve AGREE reference errors; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
Do not use invalid scalar type names like 'integer' or 'boolean' in AGREE const/eq declarations.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about component type annexes
